tasks.title: Count Vowels in a String
tasks.description:
In C programming, a string is fundamentally an array of characters, terminated by a special null character (`\0`). Understanding how to process strings is crucial for many applications, from parsing user input to text analysis and data manipulation. This exercise will help you get familiar with iterating through strings and performing character-based checks, a fundamental skill for text processing.

Your task is to write a C program that reads a single line of text from standard input and counts the total number of vowels present in it. Vowels are defined as 'a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u', and the counting should be case-insensitive (meaning 'A' counts as a vowel, just like 'a'). The input string will not exceed 100 characters in length. Your program should print the final count of vowels, followed by a newline. If the input string contains no vowels, the count should be 0.
